description: DID commands — create, update, delete, and query on-chain Decentralized Identifiers on the XRP Ledger

userStories.title: did set command
userStories.description: As an identity provider, I want to publish or update a DID on-chain from the CLI so I can associate identity credentials with my XRPL account.
acceptanceCriteria: Read DIDSet docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/didset.md before implementing
Create src/commands/did.ts and register it in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts

xrpl did set with: --uri <string> (auto hex-encoded), --uri-hex <hex> (mutually exclusive with --uri), --data <string> (auto hex-encoded), --data-hex <hex> (mutually exclusive with --data), --did-document <string> (auto hex-encoded), --did-document-hex <hex> (mutually exclusive with --did-document)
--clear-uri, --clear-data, --clear-did-document flags to clear individual fields (sends field as empty string)
Passing --uri '' (empty string) is equivalent to --clear-uri
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
CLI exits with error if none of the set/clear flags are provided
CLI exits with error if both --uri and --uri-hex are provided (same for data and did-document pairs)
CLI exits with error if both a value flag and its corresponding clear flag are provided for the same field (e.g. --uri and --clear-uri together)
tests/e2e/did/did.validation.test.ts: no fields provided, conflicting plain/hex flags, conflicting set/clear for same field (no network)
tests/e2e/did/did.set.test.ts: create DID with --uri, create with --data, update URI, clear a field with --clear-uri; --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: did delete and did get commands
userStories.description: As an identity provider, I want to delete my DID and query DID data so I can manage my on-chain identity lifecycle and verify published data.
acceptanceCriteria: Read DIDDelete docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/diddelete.md before implementing
xrpl did delete subcommand with standard key material and output options only (no DID-specific flags)
xrpl did get <address> subcommand using account_objects RPC with type: 'did'
did get default output: URI (decoded from hex to UTF-8), Data (shown as raw hex), DIDDocument (decoded from hex to UTF-8)
did get shows 'No DID found for <address>.' if account has no DID
--json outputs raw JSON ledger entry
--node supported on did get
tests/e2e/did/did.delete.test.ts: create DID, delete it, verify did get returns not-found after deletion; --json, --dry-run
tests/e2e/did/did.get.test.ts: create DID with URI and data fields, verify did get returns correct decoded values; --json
Typecheck passes
